reforms.description: The Canadian government extended the investment tax credit scheme, broadening its scope and increasing benefits to encourage investment in slower-growth regions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging investment in slower-growth regions is expected to stimulate economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into effect as part of the government's strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly broadens the existing scheme, making it a notable change without being a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: This provision was extended for a further year in October 1977.

reforms.description: A new line of action was introduced with the granting of employment credits to business firms, providing income tax credits to employers hiring designated persons, aimed at creating new job opportunities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing tax credits to employers is designed to incentivize hiring and stimulate job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented to create new job opportunities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of employment credits represents a significant but not transformative change in employment policy.
reforms.source_quote: A new line of action in this field was introduced in April this year with the granting of employment credits to business firms.
